commonIssues: Flowering slows or stops in hot summer weather; plants look ragged but usually revive in cooler fall temperatures (shear to encourage rebloom)
Stems can rot in overly wet or crowded conditions; avoid overwatering and ensure good drainage
Susceptible to downy mildew, clubroot, white blister, slugs, and flea beetles
Heavy fertilization promotes foliage at the expense of flowers; feed sparingly
Deadhead or clip over after the first flush of bloom to encourage further flowering

source.citation: University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension Horticulture, 'Sweet Alyssum, Lobularia maritima' — full sun in cool climates, part shade in hot climates; tolerates short dry periods but best with consistent moisture; avoid overwatering (rot in wet/crowded conditions); fertilize sparingly; mat-forming 3-9 in. tall; ideal for containers and hanging baskets; flowering slows in heat and revives in fall.
University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension Horticulture, 'Sweet Alyssum, Lobularia maritima' — full sun (cool climates) to partial shade (hot climates); basis for 6 h target / 4 h minimum sun.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, 'Lobularia maritima' (Kemper Code a103) — full sun to part shade; water dry to medium, tolerates drought and dry soil; zones 5-9 (grown as annual); blooms spring to frost in cool-summer climates; declines in summer heat, revives in fall; 0.25-0.75 ft tall, 0.5-1 ft wide; suitable for mixed containers; no serious insect or disease problems.
Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, 'Lobularia maritima' — dry-to-medium water needs and drought tolerance; basis for medium dryingTolerance.

Clemson Cooperative Extension Home & Garden Information Center, 'Sweet Alyssum' — sunny location with some afternoon shade; fertilize monthly with all-purpose fertilizer through the growing season (basis for 30-day feeding interval); excels in pots, hanging baskets and window boxes; plant several weeks before the last spring frost; September sowing flowers fall through winter in mild climates.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, 'Sweet Alyssum' — container, hanging-basket and window-box suitability; cascading habit over edges (basis for trailing growthHabit and verticalSuitability 0.8).

Royal Horticultural Society, 'Lobularia maritima' — hardiness H3 (minimum -5 to 1 C; basis for coldHardyMinC -5); full sun; light, moderately fertile, well-drained soil, any pH; up to 10 cm tall, 10-50 cm spread; suitable for patios and containers; pests/diseases: downy mildew, clubroot, white blister, slugs, flea beetles; clip over after first flush to encourage further flowering.
Royal Horticultural Society, 'Lobularia maritima' — light, moderately fertile, well-drained soil across chalk/clay/loam/sand; basis for soilMixTags.

Confidence note: sources give qualitative guidance ('consistent moisture', 'dry to medium', 'fertilize monthly', 'declines in summer heat'). Numeric conversions are conservative estimates: baseIntervalDays 2 (container annual needing consistent moisture), hotWeatherMultiplier 0.5, rainSkipThresholdMm 6, container liters 2/4-10 (small shallow-rooted 8-23 cm plant; standard 15-25 cm pot or basket), heatStressC 29 (flowering decline reported in hot summers), maxSunHours 10. No source states these exact numbers.
